channel.title: OpSec on Project Sentry | Threat Intel
channel.description: Recent content in OpSec on Project Sentry | Threat Intel

item.title: Day 4: The Predator/Prey Matrix, Malicious Tooling, and the 'Fake Fullz' Economy
item.description: The Threat Landscape: The Predator/Prey Matrix Today’s intelligence originates from a veteran operator inside a gray-market forum, issuing a warning to the community’s novices. The core revelation is a structural truth about illicit networks: 95% of the participants are not actual operators; they are the liquidity. The post exposes the reality of “Carding” and US financial aid fraud (“HELB ya Yues”). Because institutional defenses like Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) and biometric verification have drastically raised the technical barrier to entry, the actual “Sharp Boys” do not sell their zero-day methods or highly guarded “Fullz” (PII data packages).

item.title: Day 2: Identity Arbitrage, 'Hidden Gem' Scams, and the Psychology of FOMO
item.description: The Threat Landscape: Identity Arbitrage Today’s threat intelligence originates from gray-market forums where users are coordinating the mass creation and exploitation of US-based AI training accounts (e.g., Oneforma, Outlier, Atlas Capture). The underlying mechanism here is Identity Arbitrage. Because centralized Web2 platforms enforce strict geographical pay disparities and tax jurisdictions, opportunists utilize VPNs, residential proxies, and forged KYC (Know Your Customer) documents to spoof their geographic location and capture higher yields.

item.title: Day 1: The 'Long Game' Airdrop Phishing Funnel & Cross-Chain Data Harvesting
